verification_method: Live fetches of modelcontextprotocol/registry docs (main branch), live registry API probes, mcp 1.27.2 wheel inspection, and end-to-end FastMCP smoke tests (stdio + streamable-http) run in the shearline venv.

registry.status: Registry is in PREVIEW; docs warn breaking changes or data resets may occur before GA.
registry.docs_layout_note: Publishing guides live in docs/modelcontextprotocol-io/*.mdx (quickstart, package-types, authentication, versioning); server.json spec lives in docs/reference/server-json/ (generic-server-json.md, official-registry-requirements.md, draft/server.schema.json).

schema.verified_live: HTTP 200, last-modified Fri 12 Dec 2025 16:40:47 GMT, JSON Schema $id matches URL; saved as fixtures/registry_server.schema.2025-12-11.json
schema.changelog: 2025-12-11 is the latest dated release (added URL template variables for remotes). A newer 'Draft (Unreleased)' exists only at docs/reference/server-json/draft/server.schema.json.

field_constraints.name: pattern ^[a-zA-Z0-9.-]+/[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$ — exactly ONE forward slash separating namespace from server name; minLength 3, maxLength 200. Example: io.github.user/weather
field_constraints.description: minLength 1, maxLength 100 (only 100 chars!). Capabilities-focused human description.
field_constraints.version: maxLength 255; SHOULD be semver; version RANGES are rejected (^1.2.3, ~1.2.3, >=1.2.3, 1.x)
field_constraints.title: optional display name, 1-100 chars

pypi_packages.registryBaseUrl: https://pypi.org (optional in package entry; official registry allows ONLY https://pypi.org for pypi)
pypi_packages.runtimeHint: uvx — string hint; schema examples are ['npx','uvx','docker','dnx']. For a plain `uvx <package>` launch you set runtimeHint:'uvx' and need NO runtimeArguments; packageArguments are appended after the package name if the CLI needs extra args.
pypi_packages.ownership_verification: Registry validator fetches the package README (PyPI long_description) and looks for the literal string 'mcp-name: <server-name>'. May be inside an HTML comment, e.g. <!-- mcp-name: io.github.lostnumber07/shearline -->. The token MUST be followed by a boundary (newline, whitespace, HTML tag, or -->); a trailing period glued to the name breaks the match.
pypi_packages.note: PyPI has no package.json 'mcpName' equivalent — verification is README-string-based only.

namespace_rules.github_auth: With GitHub-based auth the server name MUST be io.github.<username>/* or io.github.<orgname>/* (lowercase 'io.github.' prefix + GitHub login). E.g. io.github.lostnumber07/shearline. Validation: mcp-publisher login github runs a GitHub device-code OAuth flow (https://github.com/login/device); the resulting registry JWT only authorizes publishing names under io.github.<authenticated-login>/.
namespace_rules.domain_auth: Alternative: reverse-DNS namespaces (com.example.*/*) proven via DNS TXT record at the domain APEX ('v=MCPv1; k=ed25519; p=<pubkey>') or HTTP file at /.well-known/mcp-registry-auth. Not needed for SHEARLINE.
namespace_rules.error_if_mismatched: "You do not have permission to publish this server" => name prefix does not match the authenticated namespace.

meta_field.rule: In server.json, ONLY _meta['io.modelcontextprotocol.registry/publisher-provided'] is preserved; any other _meta keys are silently dropped. Hard 4KB (4096 bytes) JSON size limit on that extension.
meta_field.api_responses: Registry API responses wrap each entry as {server: {...}, _meta: {'io.modelcontextprotocol.registry/official': {status, publishedAt, updatedAt, isLatest, statusChangedAt}}} — registry-managed _meta lives at the RESPONSE level under the .../official key (observed live), distinct from publisher-provided _meta inside server.

publish_workflow: 1. Add '<!-- mcp-name: io.github.lostnumber07/shearline -->' to the package README (becomes PyPI description)
2. Publish package to PyPI first (registry hosts metadata only, never artifacts)
3. Install mcp-publisher (brew install mcp-publisher, or release tarball from github.com/modelcontextprotocol/registry/releases: mcp-publisher_<os>_<arch>.tar.gz)
4. mcp-publisher init  (generates server.json template; uses camelCase keys: registryType, environmentVariables, etc.)

live_api_observations.legacy_v0: https://registry.modelcontextprotocol.io/v0/servers still returns 200 (both API versions live as of 2026-06-10)
live_api_observations.shearline_name_free: search=shearline returns {servers:[],metadata:{count:0}} — name unclaimed as of 2026-06-10

python_sdk.released_version_on_pypi: mcp 1.27.2 (requires-python >=3.10); this exact version is installed in the shearline venv and was smoke-tested.

imports.types: from mcp.types import CallToolResult, TextContent, Icon
imports.stdio_client: from mcp import ClientSession, StdioServerParameters; from mcp.client.stdio import stdio_client
imports.streamable_http_client: from mcp.client.streamable_http import streamablehttp_client  # yields (read, write, get_session_id)

fastmcp_constructor_kwargs_1_27_2.kwargs: name=None, instructions=None, website_url=None, icons=None, auth_server_provider=None, token_verifier=None, event_store=None, retry_interval=None, tools=None, debug=False, log_level='INFO', host='127.0.0.1', port=8000, mount_path='/', sse_path='/sse', message_path='/messages/', streamable_http_path='/mcp', json_response=False, stateless_http=False, warn_on_duplicate_resources=True, warn_on_duplicate_tools=True, warn_on_duplicate_prompts=True, dependencies=(), lifespan=None, auth=None, transport_security=None
fastmcp_constructor_kwargs_1_27_2.host_port: YES — FastMCP(host=..., port=...) works directly; also reachable via instance.settings.host/.port; Settings is a pydantic BaseSettings with env prefix FASTMCP_ (e.g. FASTMCP_PORT=9000) and .env file support.

run_api.signature: mcp.run(transport: Literal['stdio','sse','streamable-http'] = 'stdio', mount_path: str | None = None) -> None  (synchronous; wraps anyio.run)
run_api.streamable_http: mcp.run(transport='streamable-http')  # serves uvicorn on settings.host:settings.port at path settings.streamable_http_path (default /mcp)
run_api.sse_status: NOT removed in 1.27.2, but README states: 'SSE transport is being superseded by Streamable HTTP transport'. Use streamable-http for anything new.
run_api.production_recommendation: README: streamable HTTP is the recommended production transport; use stateless_http=True and json_response=True for optimal scalability.
run_api.asgi_mounting: mcp.streamable_http_app() returns a Starlette app to Mount(); requires running mcp.session_manager.run() in the outer lifespan; mounted path becomes <mount>/mcp unless settings.streamable_http_path='/'.
run_api.cors_note: Browser clients need CORSMiddleware with expose_headers=['Mcp-Session-Id'].

tool_declaration.decorator: @mcp.tool()  — NOTE the parentheses; decorator params: name, title, description, annotations, icons, meta, structured_output (all optional)
tool_declaration.typed_params: Plain Python type hints on the function generate inputSchema via pydantic (verified: def add(a: int, b: int) -> int produced required ['a','b'] integer properties).
tool_declaration.context_injection: Add a param annotated Context (e.g. ctx: Context) to get logging/progress: await ctx.info(...), await ctx.report_progress(progress=, total=, message=).

structured_output.default_behavior: Tools return structured results AUTOMATICALLY when the return annotation is compatible; outputSchema is generated and results are validated against it. Unstructured text content is ALSO returned for backward compat.
structured_output.supported_returns: Pydantic BaseModel, TypedDict, dataclasses/classes with type hints, dict[str, T]; primitives and generics (str/int/float/bool/list/tuple/Optional) are wrapped as {'result': value}.
structured_output.verified_live: add()->int gave structuredContent {'result': 5} (wrapped); wx()->WeatherData(BaseModel) gave structuredContent {'temperature_c': 22.5, 'condition': 'sunny'} with full outputSchema incl. Field descriptions; stats()->dict[str,float] gave outputSchema {additionalProperties:{type:number}} and exact dict back.
structured_output.full_control: Return mcp.types.CallToolResult directly (content=[TextContent(...)], structuredContent={...}, _meta={...}); must not be Optional/Union — for empty results return CallToolResult(content=[]).

smoke_test_results.stdio: fixtures/python_sdk_smoke_stdio.json — list_tools inputSchema/outputSchema + 3 tool calls, all structuredContent correct
smoke_test_results.streamable_http: fixtures/python_sdk_smoke_streamable_http.json — FastMCP(host='127.0.0.1', port=8765, stateless_http=True, json_response=True), client connected to http://127.0.0.1:8765/mcp, serverInfo {name:'smoke', version:'1.27.2'}, protocolVersion '2025-11-25'

deprecations_and_upcoming_breakage.sse: sse transport still works in 1.27.2 but is superseded by streamable-http; do not build new code on it.
deprecations_and_upcoming_breakage.fastmcp_rename_on_main: WARNING (verified 2026-06-10): python-sdk main branch has RENAMED mcp.server.fastmcp -> mcp.server.mcpserver with class MCPServer ('A more ergonomic interface for MCP servers'); src/mcp/server/fastmcp/ no longer exists on main while the README still shows FastMCP imports. The released 1.27.2 wheel still ships mcp/server/fastmcp/. PIN mcp<2 (or mcp~=1.27) in pyproject to avoid breakage when the rename ships.

server_json_skeleton_shearline.name: io.github.lostnumber07/shearline
server_json_skeleton_shearline.title: SHEARLINE
server_json_skeleton_shearline.description: Severe-weather MCP server: NWS warnings, SPC outlooks, point environments, MRMS, storm reports

skeleton_notes: Server name uses a SLASH not a dot: io.github.lostnumber07/shearline (the task phrase 'io.github.lostnumber07.shearline' would FAIL the schema pattern — exactly one '/' required, and GitHub auth requires the io.github.<login>/ prefix).
description above is 96 chars — schema caps it at 100; trim before adding anything.
identifier 'shearline-mcp' is a placeholder; both 'shearline' and 'shearline-mcp' were unclaimed on PyPI as of 2026-06-10. Whatever is chosen must match the published PyPI project name exactly.
Package 'version' should match the PyPI release version and is bumped in BOTH places (top-level + packages[0]) on every publish.
Add '<!-- mcp-name: io.github.lostnumber07/shearline -->' on its own line in README.md before the PyPI upload, or registry publish fails with 'Registry validation failed for package'.
If env vars are needed later, add packages[0].environmentVariables: [{name, description, isRequired, isSecret, default, format}].
